name: Corn & Tomato Polenta
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy, 3-quart saucepan, bring water and salt to a boil.
2. Slowly pour cornmeal into the saucepan while the water is still boiling, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
3. Reduce heat and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring often until the mixture becomes stiff.
4. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan, heat tomato sauce, oregano, corn, hot pepper flakes, and pepper to taste.
5. When the cornmeal is stiff, turn half into a serving dish and top with half the sauce.
6. Layer remaining cornmeal and sauce on top and let rest for 5 to 10 minutes.
7. Cut into squares and serve.
Food Exchanges per serving: 1 1/2 STARCH/BREAD Exchange
CAL: 105, CHO: Omg; CAR: 23g; PRO: 3g; SOD: 208mg; FAT: Og;
Source: Light & Easy Diabetics Cuisine by Betty Marks
